summary: URC Construction (P) Ltd. invokes Clause 25 of the General Conditions of Contract (GCC) to request the Department of Space to settle outstanding claims related to the construction of an extension to the Assembly Integration and Test Facility (AITF-1) at ISITE Campus in Bangalore. URC claims that the department's stance has changed throughout the project, and they are now facing financial losses due to delays, COVID-19 shutdowns, and non-payment of dues. URC requests compensation for increased costs, interest on delayed payments, and invokes Clause 25 for dispute resolution.

summary: URC Constructions Private Limited has filed a commercial suit against the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) seeking recovery of Rs. 26,15,91,147 due for the execution of a work order. URC claims entitlement to interest at 12% per annum from the date of the final bill submission and 18% per annum on the decretal amount until realization.

summary: URC Construction (P) Ltd. informs URSC about the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and subsequent lockdown imposed by the Government of India on the construction project of Extension to Assembly Integration and Test Facility (AITF 1). URC anticipates consequences on contract performance and expenditures due to the restrictions and seeks consideration for the suspension's impact on time and cost. The letter serves as an intimation as per the contract.

dispute_signals.description: Anticipated project delays due to COVID-19 restrictions.

summary: This Office Memorandum issued by the Department of Expenditure, Ministry of Finance, Government of India, addresses the invocation of the Force Majeure Clause (FMC) due to disruptions caused by the Coronavirus pandemic. It clarifies that the restrictions on the movement of goods, services, and manpower due to lockdown may allow parties to invoke FMC. The memorandum states that completion dates after February 20, 2020, can be extended for three to six months without penalty, provided the parties were not in default as of February 19, 2020.

dispute_signals.description: The document discusses the invocation of Force Majeure Clause (FMC) due to disruptions caused by the Coronavirus pandemic.
The document addresses delays in fulfilling contractual obligations due to restrictions on movement of goods, services, and manpower.
The document explicitly mentions the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on contractual obligations.

dates.context: No subsequent increase in Performance Security beyond this date

summary: This office memorandum from the Government of India, Ministry of Finance, addresses performance security in government contracts. It reduces the performance security from 5-10% to 3% of the contract value for all existing contracts, except those under dispute with ongoing arbitration or court proceedings. This reduction applies to tenders/contracts issued/concluded until 31.12.2021 and remains valid for the contract's duration.

dispute_signals.description: Exclusion of contracts under dispute with ongoing arbitration from reduced performance security benefit.
Addresses the quantum of Performance Security in Government contracts.
References slowdown in economy due to the pandemic as a reason for financial crunch among contractors.

summary: This document is a letter from URC Construction (P) Ltd to the Department of Space regarding the pending final bill and escalation payment for the Construction of Extension of Assembly Integration and Test Facility (AITF-I) project. The work was completed in May 2022, but the payment of Rs. 13.21 Crores is still pending. URC Construction requests an appointment with the Hon'ble Member (Finance) to discuss the financial crisis caused by the delayed payment.

dispute_signals.description: The contractor claims non-receipt of payment for completed work.
The contractor is escalating the payment issue to higher authorities.
Delay in payment is causing financial crisis for the contractor.

summary: This letter from the Department of Space addresses URC Construction's queries regarding the final extension of time (EOT) for the Assembly Integration & Test facility (AITF-1) construction project. It responds to URC's letters and reiterates that the final EOT was granted as per agreement conditions, considering justified and unjustified delays. The letter clarifies the department's position on price escalation and compensation, referencing clause 2A and schedule 'F' of the agreement.

dispute_signals.description: The document discusses delays in the construction project and the allocation of responsibility for these delays.
The document concerns the grant of a final extension of time (EOT) and the disputes surrounding it.
The contractor claims entitlement to price escalation for the entire extended period, which is disputed by the department.
The department's decision to levy compensation for delays is contested by the contractor.
The contractor is awaiting the release of the final bill amount and final escalation bill, which is being disputed.

dates.context: Representation submitted against non-payment of final bill

summary: URC Construction Private Limited filed a writ petition in the High Court of Karnataka against the Secretary, Department of Space, and the Chairman, Indian Space Research Organisation, seeking a directive to release payments against a final bill submitted on 22.02.2023 and 06.09.2023. The petitioner claims completion of a construction project and non-payment of the final bill. The court allowed the writ petition and directed the Chairman of ISRO to consider the petitioner's representation and pass appropriate orders within eight weeks.

dispute_signals.description: The petitioner claims non-payment of the final bill for a completed construction project.
